message:
test(mux): add B23d-f external behavioral contract tests for Cx cancellation paths

Add three new cross-layer integration tests (B23d, B23e, B23f) that
exercise the public DirectMuxClient API from an external test crate,
validating Cx-aware cancellation behavior end-to-end. All tests are
gated on feature = "asupersync-runtime".

Shared test infrastructure additions:
- cancelled_test_cx(): Creates a pre-cancelled Cx with zero poll quota
  and User cancellation kind
- assert_cancelled_mux_io(): Validates that a DirectMuxError is an Io
  error with Interrupted kind and message containing "cancelled"

B23d (connect cancellation):
- Verifies pre-cancelled Cx prevents socket connection from opening.
  Mock server listens but asserts no connection was accepted. Emits
  ABC-CAN-002 behavioral evidence.

B23e (request cancellation):
- Establishes a real handshake, waits for synchronization signal, then
  sends ListPanes with pre-cancelled Cx. Verifies no post-handshake
  request bytes reach the server. Uses handshake_seen channel barrier
  to eliminate the race condition fixed in the prior commit.

B23f (response-read cancellation):
- Sends a real ListPanes request with valid Cx, then reads the response
  with a pre-cancelled Cx. Verifies the response read fails before
  blocking, while the server confirms it received exactly one request.

Also updates test section header from B21-B23c to B21-B23f and adds
Budget and Cx to the cfg-gated import list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
